en: Please select the option that most closely resembles your actual experience.
ko: 가장 현실적으로 와닿거나, 실제 당신의 경험과 가장 가까운 것을 선택해 주세요.

en: Read the narrative and choose the image or reaction that immediately draws you in.
ko: 머리로 생각하지 말고, 본능적으로 끌리거나 가장 강렬하게 다가오는 심상을 선택해 주세요.

en: Recall the time when the world first began to feel a little wider.
Beyond home and family — when you first spent days among your peers.
Choose the sentence that feels closest to the mood you often lived in then.
ko: 세상이 처음으로 조금 넓게 느껴졌던 시절을 떠올려보세요.
집과 가족만이 아니라, 처음으로 또래들과 함께 지내던 그 무렵—
그때 가장 자주 느껴지던 정서에 가까운 문장을 골라주세요.

en: Emotions weren’t loud or vivid then.
It was more like watching the world from a quiet distance.
Not reaching out or taking —
just breathing in the still air of faraway things.
ko: 조금 떨어진 자리에서 세상을 지켜보던 시기.

en: I felt a gentle warmth moving between people.
Even if not always loved, there was a softness
in the air whenever hearts touched or stayed close.
ko: 사람들 사이 감정의 따뜻한 흐름을 느꼈다.
마음이 닿고 함께 있으면 부드러워지는 공기.

en: I became aware of the sense of ‘me.’
A wish to do well, or to be seen that way.
The quiet urge to find the color of my own being.
ko: 잘하고 싶거나 잘 보이고 싶은 마음이 있었다.

en: I was surrounded by the familiar,
and my heart felt mostly calm.
An emotion that breathed within care and known echoes.
ko: 돌봄과 익숙한 울림 속에서 숨 쉬는 감정.

en: The world seemed to press against me.
There was a sense of power gathering inside.
An undercurrent of endurance — or the need to push forward.
ko: 버티거나 나아가야 하는 기운이 배경에 깔렸다.

en: The world felt wide open,
a mix of fear and excitement — a hunger to know more.
An air of unseen worlds calling from beyond.
ko: 세상이 더 넓게 열려 있는 듯했고,
겁과 흥분이 섞인, '더 알고 싶음'이 있었다.
아직 모르는 세계가 날 부르는 공기.

en: There was a wish to understand something,
a quiet sense of what was right or true.
A subtle authority that the heart must grow.
The world wasn’t light or easy.
Caution and patience had already settled in my heart.
Even as a child, a faint gravity of adulthood seeped through.
ko: 너무 어린데도 어른스러운 무게감이 스며 있었다.

en: My mind was quick and light.
Watching, absorbing, learning —
moving with the rhythm of what surrounded me.
Among your peers, teachers, and adults—
how did the world feel to you back then?
ko: 그 시절, 당신이 체감한 세상은 어떤 느낌이었나요?

en: People were kind, yet somehow distant.
There was warmth in how they treated me,
but it always seemed to fade into empty air.
ko: 그 끝이 늘 허공에 닿는 느낌.

en: I felt welcomed, and it was easy to be around others.
Their gaze was gentle,
and kindness flowed naturally, like the air itself.
There were praises and expectations.
Eyes often turned toward me,
and the weight of those expectations filled the air.
I felt protected.
There was an atmosphere of care around me—
a shade of nurture and familiarity.
I had to be strong.
Hierarchy felt natural, even among children.
The playground was a place where strength spoke louder than words.
People were curious about me.
Sometimes they looked with fascination,
their gaze mixing curiosity with a touch of strangeness.
There was much advice and correction.
Right and wrong, rules and lessons were emphasized.
It was a gentle guidance toward what was ‘proper.’
I spent much time in quiet thought.
Calm, deliberate—
there was a weight in me that didn’t quite belong to a child.
My thoughts were quick, my awareness sharp.
I responded with wit,
always reading and adjusting to the atmosphere around me.
From a distant origin, a trace has drawn you here.
Among these, choose the one that feels closest to your ‘original self.’
Do not try to interpret—choose what you sense.
ko: 먼 기원으로부터 당신을 이곳으로 이끈 흔적이 있습니다.
이 중, 가장 “본래의 당신”에 가깝다고 느껴지는 것을 고르세요.
의미를 해석하려고 하지 말고, 감각적으로 잡히는 것을 고르세요.

en: “A Fragment of Black Mineral, Fractured”
Once whole, now a splinter broken away.
Separation · Dissolution · Afterglow of Memory.
“A Silver Ornament Plate with Faded Floral Carvings”
Traces of beauty once engraved, now blurred.
Form · Pleasure · Echo of Affection.
“A Golden Seal Fragment Holding Sunlight”
Once complete, now broken from its crest.
Honor · Will · Residue of Dignity.
“A Small Seed Husk, Clinging to Dried Soil”
The primal nourishment of matter itself.
Origin · Growth · Instinct of Life.
ko: “건조된 씨앗과 흙이 붙은 작은 종자 껍질”

en: “A Rusted Shard of Red Iron”
Remnant of a blade.
Conflict · Resistance · Strength · Struggle.
“A Black Metal Fragment Etched with Unknown Symbols”
An unreadable, magnetic code.
Destructive Curiosity · Threshold Experience · Mutation.
ko: 의미를 알 수 없는 신비로운 암호.

en: “A Fragment Bearing a Faintly Burned Celestial Mark”
Echo of cosmic pattern, remnant of scripture.
Wisdom · Law · Protection.
“A Gray Stone Eroded Like Rock Salt”
Marked by time and pressure.
Limit · Weight · Fate.
“A Fine Brass Gear Fragment”
Part of a greater unseen mechanism.
Intellect · Play · Calculation · Agility.
In the deep stillness where no one can reach you,
when the heart settles on its own,
choose the sensation that feels most familiar.
ko: 아무도 닿지 않는 깊은 고요 속에서

en: “A Fading Echo”
The space where something has already passed.
Neither heavy nor sharp—
just quietly dissolving into stillness.
“A Warmth Just Out of Reach”
A memory like body heat lingering at your fingertips.
A nameless longing brushes by—
tender, and a little aching.
ko: 손끝에 남아 있는 체온 같은 기억.

en: “The Quiet of a Closed Core”
Light sealed deep within.
There is no outside—
only the self, steady and still.
“The Trace of a Damp Breath”
A tide pooled beneath the chest.
Neither light nor heavy—
only a gentle sadness wrapping around me.
“A Tension That Doesn’t Move”
No explosion, no anger.
Only muscles drawn tight,
waiting—without reason.
“A Hollow with Thirst”
Empty, not knowing what it seeks.
Not desire—
just a space that remains.
ko: 무엇을 원하는지 모르는 채, 속이 비어 있다.

en: “A Distant Breath Observing”
It does not join in.
It does not judge.
It simply watches—calm, and wide.
The world wasn’t light or easy.
Caution and patience had already settled in my heart.
Even as a child, a faint gravity of adulthood seeped through.
“A Floating Consciousness”
Dust of thought without emotion.
Something tries to speak, then forgets—
scattering with no sound, no conclusion.
Was there a moment when the ‘atmosphere of the world’ seemed to change for you?
It doesn’t have to be from childhood.
For some, it comes around early school years;
for others, during adolescence or early adulthood.
Choose the period that feels closest to when that shift occurred.
ko: 처음으로 ‘세상의 공기’가 바뀐 것 같은 순간이 있었나요?
어떤 사람에게는 사춘기나 성인 초반에 찾아옵니다.
그 변화의 시기와 가장 가까운 느낌을 골라주세요.

child.en: The air suddenly changed when I still knew little about the world. (early childhood)
child.ko: 아직 세상에 대해 잘 모르던 때, 공기가 갑자기 달라졌다. (~초등 저학년)

puberty_start.en: It was when friendships and the rules of the world began to grow complicated. (late elementary to early adolescence)
puberty_start.ko: 친구 관계와 세상의 규칙이 복잡해지기 시작했던 시기였다. (초등 고학년~사춘기 초반)

puberty.en: It was the time when conflicts arose within me, and I began to wrestle with myself. (adolescence)
puberty.ko: 내 안에서 충돌이 생기고, 스스로와 싸우기 시작했던 시기였다. (사춘기)

post_puberty.en: It came around the time I was about to become an adult—when responsibility and choice began to fall on me. (late teens to early adulthood)
post_puberty.ko: 막 성인이 될 즈음, 스스로 선택해야 한다는 책임이 찾아오던 시기였다. (10대 후반~성년 초반)

20s.en: When I stepped into society, and the world began to press its real weight upon me. (early to mid-twenties)
20s.ko: 사회에 뛰어들며, 세상이 진짜로 무게를 실어오기 시작했던 시기였다. (20대 초중반)

no_shift.en: I haven’t really felt such a moment—or can’t recall one clearly.
no_shift.ko: 아직 그런 순간이 없거나, 잘 떠오르지 않는다.

turmoil.en: My life has always been full of upheaval; it’s hard to say one moment stood out more than the rest.
turmoil.ko: 내 인생은 매 순간이 파란만장했어서, 특별히 더 많이 변한 적이 있는지는 잘 모르겠다.

en: When that change arrived,
what color did your inner world take on?
ko: 당신의 내면은 어떤 색을 띄고 있었나요?

en: I spoke less.
Often stopped mid-thought.
A quiet emptiness opened inside the once-light heart—
as if something drifted away from afar.
Only a silent breeze remained.
I used to find it hard to talk to people,
but somehow they began to come closer.
I can’t say it was good or bad—it just happened that way.
ko: 어느 순간 사람들이 조금 더 다가왔다.
그게 좋다 나쁘다는 잘 모르겠고, 그냥 그렇게 됐다.

en: The light days grew a little heavier.
I felt eyes turning toward me,
and somehow wanted to stand properly.
It was as if a quiet light shone on me.
ko: 괜히 바르게 있어야 할 것 같았다.
조용한 빛이 나를 비춘 것 같았다.

en: The busy noise moved further away.
Being alone started to feel natural.
The world became a little quieter.
It became harder to relax like before.
There were sudden moments of tension,
and I felt I shouldn’t just stay still—
as if I was always getting ready for something.
ko: 가만히 있으면 안 될 것 같았다.

en: The time of pure strength had passed,
and too many paths opened at once.
I couldn’t stay in one place long—
new things kept catching my eye.
Even in stillness, the wind was moving.
“A Distant Breath Observing”
It does not join in.
It does not judge.
It simply watches—calm, and wide.
I spoke less.
Became cautious for no clear reason.
Did the same things again and again.
It was a time of long, quiet thinking alone.
Things felt lighter.
Small details caught my eye,
little joys appeared.
My words came a bit faster.
None of these quite fit.
I know something changed,
but the exact feeling doesn’t come to mind.
When that change arrived,
what color did your inner world take on?
ko: 당신의 내면은 어떤 색을 띄고 있었나요?

en: Things that once made sense stopped making sense.
I felt a step away from the world I thought I knew.
Confused—but strangely indifferent.
ko: 설명이 되던 것들이 설명되지 않기 시작했다.
내가 알던 세상에서 한 걸음 떨어져 있는 느낌이 들었다.

en: The world suddenly looked more complicated.
People and feelings drew my curiosity—and my confusion.
I wanted to get closer, but quietly watched instead,
afraid I might not understand.
ko: 세상이 갑자기 더 복잡해 보이기 시작했다.
다가가고 싶으면서, 이해하지 못할까봐 조용히 관찰했다.

en: I thought less about how I felt,
and more about who I should become.
More than being liked, I wanted to stand firm.
I was sensitive—but responsibility had begun to grow.
ko: 내 감정보다, ‘어떤 사람이 되어야 할까’를 더 생각했다.
나를 좋아해주길 바라는 마음보다, 제대로 서야 한다는 의지가 커졌다.

en: The voice saying ‘be strong’ quieted down,
and feelings came forward.
The wish to connect with someone grew louder.
What I felt started to matter more.
ko: 강해야 한다는 목소리가 잦아들고, 감정이 앞섰다.
누군가와 연결되고 싶다는 마음이 자꾸 커졌다.

en: I began searching for who I had to be,
not just who I vaguely was.
Action came before thought,
and a quiet confidence began to form.
The wish to prove myself grew.
ko: 막연한 나가 아니라, ‘나여야 하는 모습’을 찾기 시작했다.
생각보다 행동이 먼저였고, 조용한 자신감이 생겼다.

en: My goal had been clear, then the road split into many.
Excitement grew—and so did fear.
I began to want to go farther than before.
ko: 목표가 분명했는데, 갑자기 길이 여러 개로 갈라졌다.
더 멀리 나가고 싶은 마음이 생겼다.

en: Scattered thoughts began to find shape.
Instead of chasing everything,
I wanted to choose one direction.
Less possibility—more principle.
ko: 갈피 잡기 힘들던 생각들이 조금씩 정리되기 시작했다.
넓게 퍼져 있던 욕심 대신, 한 방향을 정하고 싶어졌다.

en: I began to see limits and reality
where I once saw endless potential.
Things I believed easily started to gain weight.
For the first time, I felt what effort really meant.
ko: 무한히 가능하단 생각보다, 한계와 현실을 보게 되었다.
쉽게 믿었던 것들이 무게를 갖기 시작했다.

en: The heaviness lifted a little.
Rather than carrying everything alone,
I wanted to untangle it through learning and exchange.
I started to handle problems with thought, not feeling.
ko: 스스로 다 받아내기보단, 배움과 교류를 통해 풀고 싶어졌다.

en: None of these quite fit.
I know something changed,
but the exact feeling doesn’t come to mind.
It feels as though you cried yourself to sleep.
You can’t quite remember why, only that the sorrow was deep.
When awareness returns, you find yourself sitting before a small spring.
The forest around you hums with something both wondrous and unsettling—
veiled in mist, yet lit by a dreamlike glow that guides your way forward.
ko: 정작 왜 울었는지는 기억조차 나지 않지만, 매우 슬펐던 것 같다.
문득 정신을 차려보니 당신은 작은 샘 앞에 앉아 있었다.
신비로우면서도 어딘가 섬뜩한 기분을 자아내는 숲은 안개에 뒤덮혀 있었지만,
그럼에도 몽환적인 빛이 앞길을 밝히고 있던 것이다.

en: Following the dreamlike light, you walk along the path.
Suddenly, the fog parts—and a radiant sun floods the forest.
At last, you stand before a great stone altar.
As you step closer, a mysterious voice descends from the heavens:
“O chosen one,
select an item that shall make your glory shine ever brighter.”
ko: 몽환적인 빛을 따라 당신은 길을 걸었다.
갑자기 안개가 확 젖히며 찬란한 태양이 숲 전체를 비추었고,
이로서 당신은 드디어 거대한 돌 제단을 마주하게 된 것이다.
제단 앞에 다가서니 천공에서 신비로운 목소리가 들려온다:
그대의 영광을 더욱 빛나게 할 소지품을 하나 고르도록 하여라.”

question.en: What item lies before you upon the altar?

en: “The Sage’s Staff”
A modest wooden staff. A steady power hums within its grain.
ko: 수수한 나무 지팡이. 나뭇결 속에서 묵직한 기운이 느껴진다.

en: “The Hierophant’s Robe”
A deep violet robe, steeped in quiet authority and reverence.
ko: 짙은 보랏빛 로브. 고요한 권위와 경건함이 감돈다.

en: “A Silver Crown”
An exquisitely wrought circlet, gleaming with serene beauty.
“The Old Lantern”
The oil nearly gone, yet the gentle flame refuses to die.
ko: 기름이 거의 다 된 듯하지만 은은한 불빛이 꺼지지 않는다.

en: As you’re drawn to your chosen item, a whisper reaches you:
“He only promised you glory, didn’t he?
But he never said it was for your sake.
Take this gift—something to guard you when the weight of glory begins to crush.”
The voice comes from a dark cave beyond the forest.
Inside, the narrow space shimmers faintly, lit by a moonlike glow.
Upon an altar—much like the one outside—rest several different objects.
ko: “그는 영광을 약속할 뿐이지, 너를 위한다고 한 적이 없잖아?
영광의 무게에 짖눌릴 때 너를 지켜줄 선물을 줄게”
목소리는 숲 저편의 어두운 동굴 속에서 들려오고 있었다.
좁은 동굴 안은 달빛과 같은 미약하지만 신비로운 빛으로 감돌고 있었다.
바깥에 있던 제단과 흡사한 제단 위에는 이와 별개의 물건들이 놓여져 있었다.

en: “A Shard of a Broken Mirror”
Cracked, yet it reflects a pale lunar light.
You cannot tell what it might reveal.
ko: 금이 가 있으나 희미한 달빛을 반사한다.

en: “Heavy Battle Boots”
Worn metal boots, marked with dried mud
and faint, unspoken stains.
ko: 바닥엔 마른 진흙과 희미한 얼룩이 남아 있다.

en: “A Bitten Red Apple”
A single bite mark remains.
Its sweet, fragrant scent lingers in the air.
ko: 달고 향기로운 냄새가 공기 속에 감돈다.

en: “A Tattered Gray Cloak”
Rough to the touch, yet lightweight.
It feels as though it could vanish into the dark without a trace.
ko: 어둠 속에서 흔적 없이 사라질 것만 같다.

en: And so your journey begins.
Filled with a strange zeal — a rush of righteousness and confidence — you witness it:
a giant serpent opening its jaws to devour a newborn bird.
A wave of disgust and fury rises within you.
Instinctively, you grasp something nearby and hurl it at the creature.
The moment it strikes, the serpent vanishes—like a shimmer of heat.
Was it a mirage in the desert?
As you approach the place where the serpent had been,
you find an object lying there.
It must have been a stone you threw… yet what lies before you
has taken on an entirely different form.
ko: 들뜨기도 하고 묘한 정의감과 자신감에 가득 찬 당신은 목격했다——
거대한 구렁이가 갓 태어난 새끼 새를 잡아먹으려 입을 벌리고 있는 모습을.
뭔지 모를 혐오감과 경멸이 울컥 밀려온 당신은
반사적으로 손에 집히는 무언가를 집어들어 구렁이를 향해 던졌다.
그 물체에 맞자, 구렁이는 즉시 아지랑이처럼 사라졌다.
그렇게 구렁이가 있던 자리에 다가가니 어떤 물건이 놓여 있었다.
분명 손에 잡힌 것은 돌맹이였던 것 같지만,
떨어져 있던 물체는 완전히 다른 형상을 띄고 있던 것이다.

en: “A Cracked Compass”
Its needle has lost direction, yet a soft light glows within.
No path, it seems, is ever fully closed.
ko: 바늘은 방향을 잃었지만, 은은한 빛이 깃든다.
그 어떤 길도 완전히 닫히진 않은 듯하다.

en: “A Sapling Branch”
Between rough bark, tender leaves emerge.
Small and fragile, yet brimming with life.
ko: 거친 껍질 사이로 어린 잎이 돋아난다.

en: “A Half-Filled Chalice”
In an ornate golden cup, clear water rests halfway up.
It feels both lacking and complete at once.
ko: 정교한 금잔 속, 맑은 물이 반쯤 채워져 있다.

en: “A Scorpion’s Shed Shell”
Empty, yet its form remains intact.
What was cast off is gone—something new will arise.
ko: 텅 비었지만 형체는 온전히 남아 있다.
벗어던진 것은 사라지고, 새로운 것이 생겨날 것이다.

en: As your hand reaches for the object, a mysterious voice echoes:
“Humans are slaves to the unconscious—so descend, and submerge within.”
Even with your eyes open, you can’t tell whether this is dream or waking.
You drift in a strange confusion, in a space that feels like the deep sea—
and yet like the womb’s amniotic cradle.
A soft comfort rises, whispering that you could simply let go of everything.
Then, in a fleeting moment of awareness, you reach toward the radiant light above the surface.
ko: 물건에 손을 뻗자, 신비로운 목소리와 함께 당신은 정신을 잃는다:
눈을 떴음에도 꿈인지 현실인지 몽롱한 느낌에 기묘한 혼란이 찾아온다.
마치 깊은 바다와 같지만, 모태의 양수와 같은 느낌도 드는 공간에서
당신은 모든 것을 놓아버리고 편해지고자 하는 이상한 안락함을 느낀다.
그러다 잠시 정신이 돌아온 당신은 수면 위의 찬란한 빛을 향해 손을 뻗는다.

question.en: What awaits you there, beyond the shimmering veil?
question.ko: 수면 위에 당신을 기다리고 있는 것은 무엇인가?

sun.en: “The Brilliant Solar Beam That Purifies All Evil”

judgment.en: “The Angel of Judgment — The End, and the Beginning Anew”

title.en: The Bottom of the Inner World

en: You reach toward the radiance above the surface,
but something pulls you back—down into the unknown depths.
You glance at what drags you, and see a figure with your own face,
smiling in a grotesque way.
You fall endlessly, until you arrive at the abyss.
ko: 수면 위의 찬란함에 가까워지고자 손을 뻗지만,
당신은 무언가에 의해 다시끔 미지의 물 속으로 끌어당겨진다.
당신과 동일한 형상의 존재가 기괴하게 웃고 있었다.
당신은 끝없이 밑바닥으로 추락하여 심연에 다다르게 되었다.

question.en: In that deepest place, what do you face?

en: “A Man with His Eyes Covered”
His eyes are bound in cloth.
In his hand, a dull blade swings into empty air.
Blood splatters.
Somewhere, a cry echoes—but he does not hear it.
ko: 그리고 손에는 무딘 검을 쥔 그는 허공을 향해 칼을 내리친다.
어디선가 울음소리가 나지만, 그는 듣지 못한다.

en: “Two Fallen Dogs and the Head of a Rooster”
The dogs lie still.
Flies drift lazily through the air.
From the rooster’s severed neck, a warm line of blood flows.
Morning never comes.
ko: “쓰러진 개 두 마리, 그리고 수탉의 머리”
수탉의 잘린 목에서 따뜻한 피가 한 줄 흐른다.

en: “A Being with the Face of a Black Goat”
Horns. Black fur. Crimson lips.
It smiles.
It looks at you.
It slowly approaches.
“A Woman in White Holding Her Own Head”
A white robe. A severed head in her arms.
Her eyes meet yours.
She does not move.
ko: 여인은 두 팔에 자신의 머리를 안고 있다.

en: Startled, you stumble backward.
Then, from somewhere unseen, a seductive voice whispers:
“You want this too, don’t you?
It’s all right—here, you can be honest.
Let me give you a gift you can carry back to the waking world.”
ko: 네가 현세에 가져갈 수 있는 선물을 하나 주도록 할게”

en: “Crown of the Absolute”
As ruler over all power and wealth,
the world itself shall bow before me.
ko: 세상 모든 권력과 재물의 지배자로서 모두가 내게 굴종하리라.

en: “Sword of the Transcendent”
No rival shall stand against the one who wields it.
No hand shall ever grasp even my garment’s hem.
“Perfume of Allure”
No soul shall resist my touch.
All shall beg for the taste of my love.
ko: 그 누구도 내 손길을 거부할 수 없으리.

en: If you had to release one thing from your life—
something that must truly be let go—
what would you be most ready to surrender?
(This is not an idealized wish, but a choice that reveals your real priorities.)
ko: 당신의 삶에서 단 하나를 반드시 내려놓아야 한다면,
가장 먼저 포기할 준비가 되는 것은 무엇인가요?
(이것은 이상적인 “바람”이 아니라, 당신의 진짜 우선순위를 보여주는 선택입니다.)

en: Let go of the urge to fulfill your duty,
to hold your position and do what is ‘right.’
Choose mere existence over righteousness, reason, or contribution.
ko: 해야 할 몫을 다하려는 마음을 내려놓는다.

en: Release stability, success, possession, direction, and plans.
Choose the flow of life over security or achievement.
ko: 안정, 물질, 성취, 자리, 방향성, 계획을 포기한다.
나의 자리나 현실적 안정보다는, 흘러가는 삶을 택한다.

en: Let go of love, pleasure, and emotional warmth.
Choose meaning—or simply survival—over happiness.
ko: 사랑, 즐거움, 감정적 만족, 관계적 따뜻함을 포기한다.
행복 대신 의미 또는 생존을 택한다.

en: Give up the freedom to withdraw from the world,
to detach from expectations or emotion.
Instead of leaving, choose to stay—and to remain in relation.
ko: 세상과 감정, 기대에서 한 발 물러날 자유를 포기한다.
떠나거나 끊어내기보다, 머물고 관계 맺는 삶을 택한다.

en: As you reach out toward the gift you cannot resist, you hear a divine voice:
“To the lost lamb, I shall grant a chance for salvation.”
At the sound of that celestial voice,
the greed and longing that once filled your heart
transform into sorrow and deep lament.
With your tears, the abyss and the devil’s temptation dissolve into air,
and you find yourself standing in a space where nothing remains—
a realm of pure selflessness.
Holding your aching chest, you weep.
Before your eyes, a radiant light descends.
You have reached paradise.
ko: 거부할 수 없는 선물을 향해 손을 뻗는 당신의 뒤로 신성한 목소리가 들린다:
“길 잃은 어린 양에게 구원의 기회를 주도록 하겠다”
마음을 가득 채우던 탐욕과 갈망은 슬픔과 비통함으로 변모했다.
눈물과 함께, 무저갱의 심연과 악마의 유혹은 즉시 증발하여,
당신은 아무것도 존재하지 않는 무아(無我)의 공간에 서 있게 되었다.
먹먹한 가슴을 쥔 채 눈물을 흘리는 당신의 눈 앞에 찬란한 빛이 도래한다.
